title: 5) Acupressure techniques
text:
Gently press or rub these tender points and acupoints. If the tightness is severe, you can
use acupuncture, but for the beginner practitioner, I do not recommend acupuncture on these
points. Acupressure and a gentle massage are enough.
①Start with gentle rubbing of the back side of the neck, and evaluate the tightness.
②Pinch the trapezius with a big grip and rub it. Compare both sides to see if there is
asymmetry or tightness.
③Let the patient turn his/her head to the side and slightly elevate the chin to stretch the
SCM, and pinch and rub the SCM as if you were sliding through the muscle. You
should check both the clavicular and sternal heads. Compare both sides for tightness
and size.
④This procedure is both diagnosis and treatment. It is very important to release the
tension from the muscles before starting acupuncture. Fully released muscles help the
patient relax, and this can increase the result and satisfaction and decrease the risk of
side effects.
~ !  ~
72
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
⓵ Sternocleidomastoid *2
The SCM has two origins, one is the sternum
and the other is the clavicle. It inserts into the
mastoid process behind the ear. The SCM ﬂexes
the head together and rotates the head separately.
⓶ Trapezius *2
The trapezius is a very big muscle from the
occipital bone to vertebrae, clavicle, and scapula.
The  trapezius  extends  the  head,  raises  the
shoulders and pulls it posteriorly, and moves the
scapula.
~ !  ~
73
⓵
⓶
POSTURAL MUSCLES IN
COSMETIC ACUPUNCTURE
These  are  the  postural  muscles  we  need  to  care  about  for  cosmetic
acupuncture. These muscles affect the position of the neck and head. Broken
balance of these muscles can affect the symmetry of the face as well. These are
skeletal muscles, which are constantly in tension to balance the head, which is
not an easy task because the head is heavy like watermelon, and the spine is as
ﬁne as broomstick. Imagine balancing a watermelon on the tip of a broomstick,
it must not be easy. Many muscles around the neck are involved in this task, but
here we focus on the biggest muscles for acupuncture treatment. The trapezius
and the Sternocleidomastoid (SCM) affect left and right symmetry at the front of
the body (SCM) and at the back (Trapezius). For this reason, before starting
acupuncture on the face, we should start by checking the balance of these two
muscles. Generally, gentle acupressure is applied on the tightened muscle, but
for severe tightness, we should release those muscles with acupuncture ﬁrst.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
